policy_statement: Account for Fiji's NDC in a way that promotes environmental integrity and avoids double counting.
mechanism_description: Account for the NDC in a way that promotes environmental integrity and transparency and avoids double counting, improving the credibility and consistency of emissions accounting.
primary_causal_pathway: Governance/accounting rules → ↑ MRV integrity (↓ double counting, ↑ transparency) → ↑ effective mitigation implementation/claims credibility → ↓ net GHG emissions (indirect)
causal_mechanism_detail:
(1) Change: National/subnational institutions adopt stricter GHG accounting methods for the NDC (inventory methods, registries, corresponding adjustments, reporting formats, audit/verification).  
(2) Mediating process: Improved measurement, reporting and verification (MRV) reduces errors and double counting, aligns datasets across sectors/market mechanisms, and increases accountability and comparability.  
(3) Climate-relevant outcome: More credible emissions accounting supports real (not overstated) mitigation progress, reduces the risk of “paper” reductions, and strengthens the effectiveness of mitigation policies and carbon market participation.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is improving the accuracy and integrity of emissions inventories/claims so mitigation actions and targets are tracked and enforced without double counting.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: governance/accounting improvements → higher integrity of emissions measurement and claims → enables/strengthens real-world emissions reductions via better enforcement and policy effectiveness.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: It is not Adaptation (does not reduce hazard/exposure), not Resource Efficiency (does not directly reduce energy/material inputs), and not Nature-Based Solutions (no ecosystem pathway). The central climate purpose is ensuring mitigation outcomes are real and not double-counted.  
3) Edge cases applied: Effects are largely enabling/indirect and may be framed as transparency/governance; however, the climate-relevant endpoint is still mitigation integrity (preventing overstated reductions and improving implementation).  
4) Applies to ALL instances: Any policy under this mechanism is about accounting integrity and anti–double counting; the shared dominant effect is credible mitigation accounting, regardless of sectoral scope or whether linked to carbon markets.  
5) Secondary subtype assignment is deferred: Whether a given policy primarily supports carbon markets, NDC tracking, inventory methods, or compliance is policy-evidence-dependent and should be assigned at the policy level.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: Some instances may function purely as transparency/administrative reform with no measurable emissions impact absent accompanying mitigation actions; classification remains Mitigation because the dominant intended climate-relevant effect is ensuring genuine, non-duplicative mitigation accounting and claims.
key_indicators: "account for Fiji's NDC"; "promote environmental integrity"; "transparency"; "accuracy"; "completeness"; "comparability"; "consistency"; "avoidance of double counting"
co_benefits: Improved public governance/accountability and statistical capacity (non-climate administrative benefits)
instance_edge_case_notes: Could also be interpreted as an MRV performance requirement (quality criteria for accounting), but the text is framed as an obligation/rule to follow, so classified as a regulatory mandate.
